title: piRNA Blood Test Predicts Mortality With 86% Accuracy, Opening New Longevity Pathways
description: Duke University researchers identified five piwi-interacting RNAs (piRNAs) that causally predict two-year survival in older adults with 86% accuracy -- outperforming age, lifestyle, and standard clinical measures. Published in Aging Cell, this biomarker panel could serve as a rapid surrogate endpoint for longevity interventions.

title: Harvard Experts Reframe Aging as Lifespans Increase -- Focus Shifts to Healthspan
description: Multiple Harvard researchers are challenging outdated assumptions about frailty and decline in older adults. William Mair's lab highlights that diet, exercise, and socioeconomic factors dramatically influence biological aging rates, while Nancy Donovan warns that social withdrawal accelerates cognitive decline.

title: ITP March 2026 Results: Zero New Lifespan Extenders Among 11 Compounds Tested
description: The NIA Interventions Testing Program released March 2026 results showing none of 11 new compounds extended mouse lifespan. Alpha-ketoglutarate, astaxanthin, and a statin-ARB combo all failed. Pioglitazone shortened female lifespan by 6.2%. The only validated lifespan extenders remain cheap generics: rapamycin, acarbose, canagliflozin, and 17-alpha estradiol.

title: UT Health San Antonio Launches Multi-Phase Rapamycin Dosing Study in Older Adults
description: A new NIA-funded clinical trial at UT Health San Antonio is recruiting 84 adults aged 65-90 to determine optimal rapamycin dosing for biological effects. The randomized, placebo-controlled study aims to move beyond speculation and establish evidence-based guidance for the most validated longevity compound in mammals.

title: Bryan Johnson Drops Rapamycin, Adds Lithium and NDGA in 2026 Blueprint Update
description: Bryan Johnson's 2026 Blueprint protocol shifts from fixed rules to adaptive, data-driven refinements. Key changes include taking NMN/NR six days per week instead of seven, removing rapamycin entirely, and adding low-dose lithium and NDGA for brain and cellular support.

title: piRNA Blood Test Predicts Two-Year Survival With 86% Accuracy
description: Duke University researchers identified five piRNAs that causally predict mortality in older adults, outperforming standard clinical measures. A virtual clinical trial showed meaningful survival improvements when piRNA levels were adjusted.

title: Experts Explore New Perspectives on Aging as Lifespans Increase
description: Harvard researchers challenge assumptions about old age, highlighting how diet, exercise, and social engagement dramatically influence biological aging rates and cognitive health.

title: UT Health San Antonio Launches Rapamycin Dosing Trial for Healthy Aging
description: NIA-funded trial recruiting 84 adults aged 65-90 to evaluate rapamycin dosing, safety, and long-term biological effects in a randomized, placebo-controlled study.

title: ITP March 2026: No New Lifespan Extenders Among 11 Compounds
description: NIA's Interventions Testing Program finds zero lifespan extension from 11 new compounds including alpha-ketoglutarate and astaxanthin. Pioglitazone shortened female lifespan 6.2%. Only cheap generics remain validated.

title: Global Push to Defeat Aging Faces Scientific Reality Check
description: With $8.49B invested in 2024 and projections to $8T by 2030, the longevity industry attracts scrutiny. Experts warn that much public narrative exceeds current science, while credible advances in GLP-1 agonists and reprogramming continue.

title: Bryan Johnson's 2026 Blueprint: Drops Rapamycin, Adds Lithium and NDGA
description: Johnson's updated supplement stack shifts to NMN/NR six days per week, removes rapamycin, and adds low-dose lithium and NDGA for brain function and cellular support based on continuous biomarker analysis.

title: Anti-Aging Therapy Market Eyes $420 Billion by 2030
description: The global anti-aging therapy market is projected to surpass $420B by 2030 at 8-9% CAGR, driven by cellular therapies and regenerative medicine moving beyond supplements into pharmaceutical solutions.

title: Blueprint Protocol 2026 Update: Data-Driven Refinements Over Fixed Rules
description: Bryan Johnson's 2026 protocol emphasizes organ-specific metrics, wearable data, and frequent lab panels. The shift from rigid rules to adaptive, data-driven refinement is the defining change.

title: Senolytics Pipeline in 2026: Narrowing Toward Precision
description: A Nature Aging study compared 21 senolytic agents. While early human trials show positive biological signals, clear efficacy evidence is still lacking. The field is shifting toward more selective, local, and biomarker-driven approaches.

title: COSMOS Trial: Daily Multivitamin Slows Biological Aging by ~4 Months
description: Mass General Brigham analysis of 958 participants found slower aging in all five epigenetic clocks after two years on a daily multivitamin, with greater benefits for those with accelerated biological age.

title: ARPA-H Awards $144M for PROSPR Program to Test Aging Interventions
description: Seven teams received funding to lay groundwork for Phase 3 trials of healthspan interventions. ARPA-H manager predicts the longevity landscape will look substantially different in 5 years because of PROSPR.

title: A&S Investment Holding Announces EUR 2 Billion Longevity Research Initiative
description: Plans announced to co-found one of the largest global research centers dedicated to longevity science and human DNA research, with the ambitious goal of extending human lifespan to 250 years.

title: Gut Microbiome Causally Linked to Ovarian Function and Aging
description: USC researchers showed fecal transplants from older female mice surprisingly improved ovarian function in young mice, revealing two-way communication between the ovary and microbiome that changes with age.

title: Vitamin C Inhibits ACSL4 to Alleviate Ferro-Aging in Primates
description: Steve Horvath highlights March 2026 as a strong month for longevity supplement research, following the COSMOS multivitamin trial with new data on Vitamin C's role in inhibiting ferroptosis-related aging in primates.

title: Top Longevity Startups by Fundraising: Over $13B in Venture Funding
description: Altos Labs leads at $3B, followed by Oura and Retro Biosciences at $1.2B each. Retro's $1B Series A is the largest single longevity funding round. The top 10 startups capture ~67% of total funding.

title: DeepStrataAge: New DNN Epigenetic Clock Reveals Sex-Specific Aging Dynamics
description: A deep-learning DNA methylation clock trained on 29,167 samples achieves 1.89 years MAE accuracy and uncovers phase-structured epigenetic waves with sex-specific dynamics via SHAP analysis.

title: Thymic Health Predicts All-Cause Mortality in Large Cohort Study
description: Deep learning quantifies thymic health from radiographic images in 25,031 participants. Higher thymic health linked to lower all-cause and cardiovascular mortality, and reduced lung cancer risk.

title: Sleep EEG Brain Age Index Linked to 39% Higher Dementia Risk
description: Analysis of 7,105 participants across five cohorts found each 10-year increase in sleep EEG brain age index is associated with 39% higher dementia risk, consistent across demographic adjustments.

title: RNASEK Enzyme Decline Drives Circular RNA Accumulation in Aging
description: KAIST researchers found RNASEK degrades circular RNA; its levels decline with age, causing RNA accumulation and stress granules. Overexpression extends lifespan in C. elegans and prevents premature aging in mammalian cells.

title: $80M NIH Grant Renews Long Life Family Study at WashU
description: Washington University receives $80M to continue studying 5,000+ participants from 530+ families with exceptional longevity, using new long-read sequencing to find genetic clues to healthy aging.

title: GLP-1 Brain Effects, ALS Eye Test, and Bionic Vision Roundup
description: Longevity.Technology's Week 11 news roundup covers GLP-1 receptor agonist effects on the brain, a new ALS diagnostic eye test, and advances in bionic vision technology.

title: Longevity Center Europe: Clients Aging 22% Slower Than Average
description: Over 80 individuals tested with DunedinPACE showed an average pace of 0.78 -- aging 22% slower than the general population. At least 10 clients in Poland are aging slower than Bryan Johnson's reported 0.65.

title: First Human Cellular Reprogramming Trial Cleared by FDA
description: Life Biosciences received IND clearance to test ER-100, a partial epigenetic reprogramming therapy, against glaucoma and NAION -- the first-ever human trial of reprogramming technology.

title: 11 Anti-Aging Biotech Companies Leading Longevity in 2026
description: Overview of key players including Altos Labs ($3B), Life Biosciences (FDA-cleared reprogramming trial), NewLimit (epigenetic reprogramming), Cambrian Bio ($30.8M ARPA-H award), and others.

title: Rapamycin for Longevity: Comprehensive Review of Pros, Cons, and Future
description: PEARL trial showed low-dose intermittent rapamycin is well tolerated over one year with modest biomarker changes, but long-term clinical benefits remain unestablished. Timing and dosing strategy are critical variables.
